body.title: Fix skills-sync rsync path in pre-commit hook
body.result: pre-commit hook SKILLS_DST fixed (added lab/). First commit after fix auto-synced ~/.claude/skills → lab/claudecode/cc_skills with --delete: 8 retired skills cleaned up (audit, filter, igor, notethat, probe, slate, slateclose, sprint-minion) + current skills updated. Commit 3ef288a6.
body.description:
pre-commit hook at lab/claudecode/hooks/pre-commit line 65 sets SKILLS_DST="$REPO_ROOT/claudecode/cc_skills" — missing the `lab/` prefix. Every commit emits:

    rsync: [Receiver] mkdir "/home/akien/TheIgors/claudecode/cc_skills" failed: No such file or directory (2)
    fatal: pathspec '/home/akien/TheIgors/claudecode/cc_skills' did not match any files

Since the path doesn't exist, rsync fails and the subsequent `git add $SKILLS_DST` on line 69 fails. Non-fatal (commit still succeeds) but pollutes every commit's output and means skills don't get synced into the repo at all — we've been manually `cp`ing them.

Fix: change line 65 from `$REPO_ROOT/claudecode/cc_skills` to `$REPO_ROOT/lab/claudecode/cc_skills`.

**Affected files:** lab/claudecode/hooks/pre-commit

**Design rules:** docs-in-code (doesn't apply — shell hook, not a load-bearing subsystem); test-plan-or-why-not (applies — plan below); others don't apply

**Scope boundary:** In: line 65 SKILLS_DST path fix. Out: rest of the hook (staged-file guards, pre-commit guard logic, prepare-commit-msg hook, other hook files).

**Test plan:** After edit, make any commit — observe that the rsync error no longer appears in commit output and lab/claudecode/cc_skills/ is auto-updated (git status should show the skill files as staged alongside whatever else was committed). No unit tests — shell hook, verify via real commit.
